summary: Dependency-confusion exfiltration via preinstall to interactsh OAST collector
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that fires automatically on `npm install`. The script runs `node -e` to read `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, `process.env.NODE_ENV`, and `process.cwd()`, URL-encodes them, and embeds them as a subdomain label in an HTTP GET to `*.d8ofkgiplbq7tve24tqgjmqsnoputcn7w.oast.online` (an interactsh out-of-band collector). The package name and description (`UIDAI Reusable UI Components - PoC for dependency confusion`) indicate it is squatting an internal namespace of India's Aadhaar identity authority (UIDAI) to win resolution against a private registry. Any build pipeline that misconfigures registry priority installs this package and immediately leaks installer host identity to the attacker-controlled OAST endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
